judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and free cash flow, but currently unprofitable on an operating and net basis with significant leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 17.8%; next earnings date is 2026-05-27.
positives: Revenue growth 30.1% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Free cash flow $1.59B TTM — positive cash generation.
Analyst growth estimates are robust for current Q (33.3%) and FY (43.5%).
Consensus rating is 'buy' with a high mean target price of $229.66.
negatives: Operating margin -33.2% and Profit margin -28.4% — the company is not yet profitable.
Return on equity -53.9% and Return on assets -9.9% — negative returns on capital.
Forward P/E 68.30x — valuation remains very high.
Debt/Equity 142.46% — significant leverage on the balance sheet.

news.paragraph: Snowflake's Q1 results are anticipated to show upside, according to RBC Capital, driven by strong business checks and increasing adoption of its Cortex Code. The company is set to report earnings on May 27th, shortly after the market reopens from the Memorial Day holiday. Traders should consider the analyst's positive outlook and the upcoming earnings catalyst.

headlines.title: Stock Market To Close For Memorial Day. Here Are Wall Street's Holiday Hours.
Snowflake (SNOW)’s Q1 Upside Likely Given Strong Checks And Ramping Cortex Code Adoption, Says RBC Capital
